def: Kinesin is a microtubule-associated force-producing molecular motor protein that transports numerous organelles along mirotubules. Kinesin is an oligomeric complex composed of two heavy chains and two identical light chains. The light chain has been proposed to function in the coupling of cargo to the heavy chain or in the modulation of its ATPase activity. The specificity of kinesin-cargo binding is thought to depend on the type of light chain that a kinesin molecule contains, where different…

label: Chlorosome envelope protein H, CsmH
def: This entry contains proteins with a repeated sequence element that is restricted to species of the Chlorobiaceae.

label: Filamin/ABP280 repeat-like
def: The many different actin cross-linking proteins share a common architecture, consisting of a globular actin-binding domain and an extended rod. Whereas their actin-binding domains consist of two calponin homology domains (see ), their rods fall into three families. The rod domain of the family including the Dictyostelium discoideum (Slime mould) gelation factor (ABP120) and human filamin (ABP280). It is constructed from tandem repeats of a 100-residue motif that is glycine and proline rich. The…

label: Cysteine-rich Golgi apparatus protein 1 repeat, eukaryote
def: This entry is found in Golgi apparatus protein 1 (GLG1, ) and related proteins in the metazoa. GLG1 is located in Golgi cisterns of various cell types, can bind fibroblast growth factor and E-selectin. Sixteen cysteine-rich GLG1 repeats form the core of the protein and are located in the lumen. The C-terminal part of GLG1 is composed of a transmembrane region and a short cytoplasmic tail. The Cys-rich GLG1 repeat is a ~60 amino acid module that contains 4 Cys residues, which can form intrachain…

label: Thrombospondin, type 3 repeat
def: Thrombospondins are multimeric multidomain glycoproteins that function at cell surfaces and in the extracellular matrix milieu. They act as regulators of cell interactions in vertebrates. They are divided into two subfamilies, A and B, according to their overall molecular organisation. The subgroup A proteins TSP-1 and -2 contain an N-terminal domain, a VWFC domain, three TSP1 repeats, three EGF-like domains, TSP3 repeats and a C-terminal domain. They are assembled as trimer. The subgroup B thr…

label: Mitochondrial carrier protein, transmembrane region
def: This entry represents the transmembrane region in mitochondrial carrier proteins. A variety of substrate carrier proteins involved in energy transfer are found in the inner mitochondrial membrane or are integral to the membranes of other eukaryotic organelles such as the peroxisome. Such proteins include: ADP/ATP carrier protein (ADP/ATP translocase) 2-oxoglutarate/malate carrier protein Phosphate carrier protein Tricarboxylate transport protein (or citrate transport protein) Graves disease car…

label: Spectrin/alpha-actinin
def: Spectrin repeats are found in several proteins involved in cytoskeletal structure and in proteins with a more regulatory role mostly in animals. This entry includes spectrin alpha and beta subunits, alpha-actinin and dystrophin.

label: Gas vesicle C repeat
def: Gas vesicles are small, hollow, gas-filled protein structures found in several cyanobacterial microorganisms. They allow the positioning of the bacteria at the favourable depth for growth. Gas vesicles are hollow cylindrical tubes, closed by a hollow, conical cap at each end. Both the conical end caps and central cylinder are made up of 4-5 nm wide ribs that run at right angles to the long axis of the structure. Gas vesicles seem to be constituted of two different protein components: GVPa and G…

label: Cell wall/choline-binding repeat
def: The cell wall-binding repeat (CW) is an about 20 amino acid residue module, essentially found in two bacterial Gram-positive protein families; the choline binding proteins and glucosyltransferases ( ). In choline-binding proteins cell wall binding repeats bind to choline moieties of both teichoic and lipoteichoic acids, two components peculiar to the cell surface of Gram-positive bacteria. In glucosyltransferases the region spanning the CW repeats is a glucan binding domain. Several crystal str…

label: Pyrrolo-quinoline quinone beta-propeller repeat
def: Pyrrolo-quinoline quinone (PQQ) is a redox coenzyme, which serves as a cofactor for a number of enzymes (quinoproteins) and particularly for some bacterial dehydrogenases. A number of bacterial quinoproteins belong to this family. Enzymes in this group contain repeats composing a β propeller.

label: Hemopexin-like repeats
def: Hemopexin ( ) is a serum glycoprotein that binds haem and transports it to the liver for breakdown and iron recovery, after which the free hemopexin returns to the circulation. Hemopexin prevents haem-mediated oxidative stress. Structurally hemopexin consists of two similar halves of approximately two hundred amino acid residues connected by a histidine-rich hinge region. Each half is itself formed by the repetition of a basic unit of some 35 to 45 residues. Hemopexin-like domains have been fou…

label: Annexin repeat
def: The annexins (or lipocortins) are a family of proteins that bind to phospholipids in a calcium-dependent manner. The 12 annexins common to vertebrates are classified in the annexin A family and named as annexins A1-A13 (or ANXA1-ANXA13), leaving A12 unassigned in the official nomenclature. Annexins outside vertebrates are classified into families B (in invertebrates), C (in fungi and some groups of unicellular eukaryotes), D (in plants), and E (in protists). Annexins are absent from yeasts and…

label: Ish1/Msc1-like
def: Proteins containing this repeat include the stress response protein Ish1 from fission yeasts and meiotic sister chromatid recombination protein 1 (Msc1) from budding yeasts. Proteins containing this repeat also include some uncharacterised proteins from Acinetobacter baumannii. Ish1 has a role in maintaining cell viability during stationary phase induced by stress response. It can be activated by the spc1 MAPK pathway.

label: Seminal vesicle protein repeat
def: The rat seminal vesicle contains six major androgen-dependent secretory proteins referred to as SVS I-VI. The SVS I-III proteins appear to be components of the rat copulatory plug, with the SVS II protein being the major component. This entry represents a repeat that is found in seminal vesical proteins.

label: Pseudomurein-binding repeat
def: Methanothermobacter thermautotrophicus (Methanobacterium thermoformicicum) is a methanogenic Gram-positive microorganism with a cell wall consisting of pseudomurein. This repeat specifically binds to pseudomurein. This repeat is found at the N-terminal of PeiW and PeiP which are pseudomurein binding phage proteins.

label: Trappin protein transglutaminase-binding repeat
def: Trappin-2,a protease inhibitor, has a unique N-terminal domain that enables it to become cross-linked to extracellular matrix proteins by transglutaminase. This domain contains several repeated motifs (represented by this entry) with the consensus sequence Gly-Gln-Asp-Pro-Val-Lys, and these together can anchor the whole molecule to extracellular matrix proteins, such as laminin, fibronectin, beta-crystallin, collagen IV, fibrinogen, and elastin, by transglutaminase-catalysed cross-links. The wh…

label: Histidine-rich calcium-binding
def: This entry represents a histidine-rich calcium-binding repeat which appears in proteins called histidine-rich-calcium binding proteins (HRC). HRC is a high capacity, low affinity Ca2+-binding protein, residing in the lumen of the sarcoplasmic reticulum. HRC binds directly to triadin. This binding interaction occurs between the histidine-rich region of HRC and multiple clusters of charged amino acids, named KEKE motifs, in the lumenal domain of triadin. This repeat is found in the acidic region…

label: Micronemal adhesive repeat, sialic-acid binding
def: This entry represents a novel carbohydrate-binding domain found on micronemal proteins. Micronemal proteins (MICs) are released onto the parasite surface just before invasion of host cells and play important roles in host cell recognition, attachment and penetration. Toxoplasma gondii can infect and replicate within all nucleated cells. This domain interacts with sialylated oligosaccharides; the protein in T. gondii is a monomer but several MAR domains are carried on the protein. Each MAR domai…

label: Stress-induced protein, KGG, repeat
def: This repeat contains a highly conserved, characteristic sequence motif, KGG, that is recognised by plants and lower eukaryotes. Further downstream from this motif is a Walker A, nucleotide binding motif. YciG is expressed as part of a three-gene operon, yciGFE and this operon is induced by stress and is regulated by RpoS, which controls the general stress-response in E coli. YciG was shown to be important for stationary-phase resistance to thermal stress and in particular to acid stress.

label: Tetratricopeptide repeat
def: The tetratricopeptide repeat region (TPR) is a structural motif present in a wide range of proteins. It mediates protein-protein interactions and the assembly of multiprotein complexes. The TPR motif consists of 3-16 tandem repeats of 34 amino acid residues, although individual TPR motifs can be dispersed in the protein sequence. Sequence alignment of the TPR domains reveals a consensus sequence defined by a pattern of small and large amino acids. TPR motifs have been identified in various diff…

label: PAC1/LIS1-like, WD-40 repeat
def: This entry represents the WD-40 repeats in PAC1/LIS1 receptors, a class B G-protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) and similar GPCR related proteins. This entry also includes small ribosomal subunit proteins RACK1 and other scaffolding proteins such as CIAO1 and MDV1. WD-40 repeats (also known as WD or β-transducin repeats) are short ~40 amino acid motifs, often terminating in a Trp-Asp (W-D) dipeptide. WD40 repeats usually assume a 7-8 bladed β-propeller fold, but proteins have been found with 4 to 1…

label: Prion, copper binding octapeptide repeat region
def: Prion protein (PrP-c) is a small glycoprotein found in high quantity in the brain of animals infected with certain degenerative neurological diseases, such as sheep scrapie and bovine spongiform encephalopathy (BSE), and the human dementias Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease (CJD) and Gerstmann-Straussler syndrome (GSS). PrP-c is encoded in the host genome and is expressed both in normal and infected cells. During infection, however, the PrP-c molecule become altered (conformationally rather than at the…

label: Fibronectin-binding repeat, SSURE
def: Streptococcal surface repeat domain - SSURE - is a protein fragment found to bind to extracellular matrix protein fibronectin but not to collagen or submaxillary mucin in Streptococci. Anti-SSURE antibodies recognised the corresponding protein on the surface of streptococcal cells. The full-length proteins are thus fibronectin-binding surface adhesins. The proteins are further characterised by having an N-terminal motif resembling [YF]SIRKxxxGxxS[VIA] and a C-terminal LPXTG motif-containing reg…

label: HEAT, type 2
def: The HEAT repeat is a tandemly repeated, 37-47 amino acid long module occurring in a number of cytoplasmic proteins, including the four name-giving proteins huntingtin, elongation factor 3 (EF3), the 65 kDa alpha regulatory subunit of protein phosphatase 2A (PP2A) and the yeast PI3-kinase TOR1. Arrays of HEAT repeats consists of 3 to 36 units forming a rod-like helical structure and appear to function as protein-protein interaction surfaces. It has been noted that many HEAT repeat-containing pro…

label: Putative metal-binding motif
def: The presence of conserved cysteine residues and the lack of hydrophobic residues suggests that this repeat might be a metal-binding site, perhaps for zinc or calcium ions.

label: Early growth response protein 1, C-terminal
def: EGR1 is a transcriptional regulator that binds specifically to 9-bp target sequences containing two CpG sites that can potentially be methylated at four cytosine bases.

label: Plasmodium repeat
def: This repeat is found in plasmodia. This entry contains two copies of the repeat. The function of these proteins is unknown.

label: Clathrin, heavy chain, propeller repeat
def: This entry represents the propeller repeat found in clathrin heavy chains. The N terminus of the heavy chain is known as the globular domain, and is composed of seven repeats which form a β propeller. Proteins synthesized on the ribosome and processed in the endoplasmic reticulum are transported from the Golgi apparatus to the trans-Golgi network (TGN), and from there via small carrier vesicles to their final destination compartment. These vesicles have specific coat proteins (such as clathrin…

label: Surface-anchored repeat, actinobacteria
def: This entry describes a region that occurs from one to three times in proteins from Actinobacteria, some of which have LPXTG-type sortase recognition motifs for covalent attachment to the Gram-positive cell wall. The region is now recognized as a "choice-of-anchor" domain, since multiple members can be found anchored by sortases (e.g. LPXTG proteins) or exosortases (e.g. PEP-CTERM proteins).

label: Parallel beta-helix repeat-2
def: This model represents a tandem pair of an approximately 22-amino acid (each) repeat homologous to the β-strand repeats that stack in a right-handed parallel β-helix in the periplasmic C-5 mannuronan epimerase, AlgA, of Pseudomonas aeruginosa. A homology domain consisting of a longer tandem array of these repeats is described in the SMART database as CASH (SM00722), and is found in many carbohydrate-binding proteins and sugar hydrolases. A single repeat is represented by SM00710. This TIGRFAMs m…

label: Cofactor-binding repeat, putative
def: This entry describes a small repeat found in a family of proteins that crosses the plasma membrane by twin-arginine translation, which usually signifies the presence of a bound cofactor. This repeat shows similarity to the β-helical repeat, in which three β-strands per repeat wind once per repeat around in a right-handed helical stack of parallel β structure.

label: Gloeobacter/Verrucomicrobia repeat
def: This entry describes a repeat originally found in proteins from some species of Verrucomicrobia (such as Chthoniobacter flavus) and in four different proteins of Gloeobacter violaceus PCC7421. In the Verrucomicrobial species, the repeat region is followed by a PEP-CTE protein-sorting signal, suggesting an extracellular location. Members of this group of proteins are found in different bacterial taxa.

label: xxxLxxG heptad repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat of seven residues each, in which the fourth residue tends to be Leu and the seventh tends to be Gly in each set. This heptad periodicity, corresponding to two turns of an α helix, suggests α-helical structure. Arrangement of these sequences on a helical wheel would show a strict alternation of Leu and Gly residues on one side of the helix, that is, an extremely bulky side chain alternating with the virtual absence of one. This suggests an extended zippering of one…

label: PXPV repeat
def: This short repeat is found in multiple copies in a variety of proteins predominantly found in proteobacteria. The function of this region is unknown.

label: YXWGXW repeat
def: This short repeat contains the motif YXWXXGXW where X can be any amino acid. It is generally found in 2-5 copies in short secreted bacterial proteins. Its function is as yet unknown.

label: Huntingtin, N-terminal, HEAT repeats 2
def: This entry represents the second set of HEAT repeats of the N-terminal domain from Huntingtin (Htt). It is separated from the rest of the domain ( ) by an unstructured region. This repeated domain is less than 40 amino acids in length and is present in huntingtin protein (Htt). Htt is expressed in many tissues, with various expression levels and is involved in a number of cellular functions. However, its exact function is not yet clear. An abnormal polyglutamine (polyQ) expansion in this protei…

label: Hemagglutinin repeat
def: This hemagglutinin repeat is found in hemolysins and filamentous hemagglutinin. Bacterial hemolysins are exotoxins that attack blood cell membranes and cause cell rupture by mechanisms not clearly defined. Filamentous hemagglutinins have a role in host-cell binding and infection.

label: Nucleoporin FG repeat
def: This entry represents a region containing FG repeats that is found in nucleoporin proteins, key elements of the selective gate-barrier that limits the diffusion of cargoes through the NPC. It includes the yeast nucleoporins Nup116, Nup100, Nup49, Nup57 and Nup 145.

label: Topoisomerase C-terminal repeat
def: This domain occurs in the C terminus of a number of DNA topoisomerases 1. It is sometimes found repeated.

label: SprB repeat
def: This repeat occurs several times in SprB, a cell surface protein involved in gliding motility in the bacterium Flavobacterium johnsoniae.

label: Leucine rich repeat 4
def: This entry represents 2 copies of a leucine rich repeat.

label: Nuclear receptor repeat
def: This is a repeat involved in dimerisation of nuclear receptors proteins and in transcriptional regulation in general. It contains a Leu-Xaa-Xaa-Leu-Leu motif which has been characterised for the orphan nuclear receptor Dax-1, which represses the constitutively expressed protein Ad4BP/SF-1. The LXXLL motif plays in important role in binding of Dax-1 to Ad4BP/SF-1.

label: Flocculin type 3 repeat
def: This repeat is found in the Flocculation protein FLO9 ( ) close to its C terminus, and in a number of other Saccharomyces proteins.

label: HEAT repeat associated with sister chromatid cohesion protein
def: This HEAT repeat is found most frequently in sister chromatid cohesion proteins such as Nipped-B. HEAT repeats are found tandemly repeated in many proteins, and they appear to serve as flexible scaffolding on which other components can assemble.

label: Modification target Cys-rich repeat
def: This entry represents a cysteine-rich repeat found in a number of bacterial putative radical-SAM modified natural product precursors.

label: Streptococcal surface-anchored protein repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat sequence that occurs primarily in LPXTG-anchored Streptococcus surface proteins, although it does occur elsewhere. It can comprise a major fraction of the length of repeat proteins that exceed 2000 amino acids in length. This repeat is flanked by collagen-like CXX motifs.

label: Cys-rich repeat, Myxococcales-type
def: This repeat is restricted to the Myxococcales, a division of the deltaproteobacteria. It occurs in several surface proteins, and may form a stalk region. The repeat averages about 21 amino acids in length with four or five Cys, three of which are nearly invariant.

label: Zinc metalloproteinase 18-residue repeat
def: This entry represents a short (18-amino acid) tandem repeat that occurs variable numbers of times in zinc metalloproteinase C (zmpC) homologues in various species of Streptococcus. This repeat occurs, oddly, as an interruption in a region of tandem repeats of another type.

label: Fn3 associated repeat
def: The entry represents a Fn3 (fibronectin type III) associated repeat. This repeat is found in several glycosyl hydrolase families of proteins such as a glycoside hydrolase that may be involved in ulvan degradation or endoglucanase, which is responsible for the endohydrolysis of (1->4)-beta-D-glucosidic linkages in cellulose, lichenin and cereal beta-D-glucans.

label: BspA-type LRR region
def: This entry represents a leucine rich repeat. A leucine-rich repeat (LRR) is a protein structural motif that forms an α/β horseshoe fold. Leucine-rich repeats are frequently involved in the formation of protein protein interactions. This repeat is found in Follicle-stimulating hormone receptors (Fshr), Thyrotropin receptors, Lutropin-choriogonadotropic hormone receptor and related proteins. Fshr are G protein-coupled receptors for follitropin, the follicle-stimulating hormone. Through cAMP produ…

label: Proline-rich tail region repeat
def: This entry describes a proline-rich tandem repeat of about 24 residues found in C-terminal regions of Gram-positive surface proteins with LPXTG sequences for processing and cell surface attachment by sortase.

label: Surface protein repeat SSSPR-51
def: This repeat domain is designated SSRS51, Streptococcal and Staphylococcal Surface Protein Repeat of size 51. Up to twelve tandem repeats can occur, on some of the longest proteins of their respective species. Nearly all member proteins carry the C-terminal sortase target sequence, LPXTG. The repeat structure and probable surface location suggest a possible adhesion function. A protein with this class of repeats may have other classes as well.

label: Surface protein repeat Ser-Ala-175
def: This serine and alanine-rich surface protein repeat, about 175 amino acids long, occurs up to nine times in surface proteins of some Lacticaseibacillus strains. Members proteins have the N-terminal variant signal sequence described by and C-terminal LPXTG signals for surface attachment by sortase.

label: Beta-propeller repeat, Msarc-type
def: This entry describes a repeat region found mostly in cell surface proteins of various methanogens. Methanosarcina barkeri, for example, has twenty such proteins, often with either seven or fourteen repeats. These repeats resemble the β-propeller repeats of the TolB periplasmic protein of Gram-negative bacteria, part of a complex associated with various functions including biopolymer transport (see ).

label: Glucan-binding repeat
def: This entry describes a region of about 63 amino acids that is composed of three repeats of a more broadly distributed family of shorter repeats represented by . While the shorter repeats are often associated with choline binding (and therefore with cell wall binding), the longer repeat described here represents a subgroup of repeat sequences associated with glucan binding, as found in a number glycosylhydrolases. A repeat consensus, WYYFDANGKAVTGAQTINGQTLYFDQDGKQVKG, has been described that cor…

label: Domain of unknown function DUF4462
def: This region is found in uncharacterised plant proteins, it is approximately 30 amino acids in length. It can be found repeated, covering the whole structure of the protein.

label: Fis1, N-terminal tetratricopeptide repeat
def: The mitochondrial fission protein Fis1 consists of two tetratricopeptide repeats. This entry represents the N-terminal tetratricopeptide repeat. Fis1 is an outer mitochondrial membrane protein that plays a role in mitochondrial membrane fission.

label: Putative flagellar system-associated repeat
def: This repeated unit can occur up to 29 times in outer membrane proteins. It is putatively associated with a novel flagellar system.

label: Fis1, C-terminal tetratricopeptide repeat
def: The mitochondrial fission protein Fis1 consists of two tetratricopeptide repeats. This entry represents the C-terminal tetratricopeptide repeat. Fis1 is an outer mitochondrial membrane protein that plays a role in mitochondrial membrane fission.

label: Lustrin, cysteine-rich repeated
def: This repeated is found in proteins from lower eukaryotes in lustrin, perlucin, pearl nacre, and other similar protein-types. Each repeat lies between Kunitz-BPTI repeats, in certain species, which are also cysteine-rich.

label: Integrase repeat unit
def: This repeat is found on integrase proteins in up to 15 copies. The function is not known.

label: CagY type 1 repeat
def: This repeat is found at the N terminus of the CagY proteins - part of the CAG pathogenicity island - and involved in delivery of the protein CagA into host cells.

label: KRBA1 family repeat
def: KRBA1 is a short repeating motif found in mammalian proteins. It is characterised by a highly conserved sequence of residues, SSPLxxLxxCLK. The function of the repeat, which can be present in up to seven copies, is unknown as is the function of the full length proteins.

label: T5SS/PEP-CTERM-associated repeat
def: This entry describes a repeat about 50 amino acids in length. Most proteins with this repeat have a C-terminal autotransporter domain ( ) and/or an N-terminal type V secretion system signal peptide ( ), while others instead have a C-terminal PEP-CTERM domain ( ).

label: ELWxxDGT repeat
def: This entry represents a protein repeat with a well-conserved motif ELWxxDGT, and a periodicity of about 48. A single protein may have as many as 18 repeats. It may consist nearly entirely of this repeat, or may have other repeats as well (e.g. hyalin repeat). It is most common in the Deltaproteobacteria.

label: Adhesin of bacterial autotransporter system
def: Adhesin involved in diffuse adherence (AIDA-I) belongs to the group of monomeric autotransporters (AT) and is an important virulence factor that mediates auto-aggregation, biofilm formation, adhesion and invasion into host cells. The AIDA repeat is found on bacterial autotransporter proteins. As the repeat is short and occurs multiple times, it is likely to be the region of the transporter that acts as the stalk between the β-barrel inserted into the membrane and the N-terminal head domain. The…

label: RHS repeat
def: RHS (rearrangement hotspot) proteins contain extended repeat regions. These repeats often appear to be involved in ligand binding. Note that the signature in this entry does not find all the repeats in a protein and that it covers two RHS repeats. The 3D structure of an RHS-repeat-containing protein (the B and C components of an ABC toxin complex) has been determined. The RHS repeats form an extended strip of β-sheet that spirals around to form a hollow shell, encapsulating the variable C-termi…

label: Signal recognition particle subunit SRP72, TPR-like repeat
def: This entry represents a TPR-like repeats found in a group of eukaryotic proteins, including signal recognition particle subunit SRP72, a component of the signal recognition particle (SRP) complex that mediates the cotranslational targeting of secretory and membrane proteins to the endoplasmic reticulum (ER). This protein binds the signal recognition particle RNA (7SL RNA) in presence of SRP68.

label: GPI-anchored cell wall protein repeat
def: This repeat is found in cell wall proteins from fungi, including some members of the HYR/IFF families.

label: Atypical Arm repeat
def: This atypical Arm repeat appears at the very C terminus of eukaryotic proteins such as importin subunit alpha, as the last of the repeating units.

label: WG containing repeat
def: This repeat contains an N-terminal WG repeat motif. The extent of the repeat is poorly defined. This repeat may form a β-solenoid structure (Bateman A pers. obs.).

label: CSPG repeat
def: The CSPG (chondroitin sulfate proteoglycan) repeat is a cadherin-like and tumor-relevant protein module which is thought to mediate interactions between cells and the extracellular matrix (ECM) in species as divergent as cyanobacteria, fly, worm, sea urchin and human. The phyletic distribution of the CSPG repeats suggests that horizontal gene transfer contributed in their evolutionary history. The CSPG domain occurs in 1-15 tandem copies per protein. In some proteins the CSPG repeat is combined…

label: Exportin-1, repeat 3
def: Chromosome region maintenance 1 / Exportin 1 mediates the nuclear transport of proteins bearing a leucin-rich nuclear export signal (NES). It contains helical repeats that are structurally similar to HEAT repeats, but share little sequence similarity with them. N-, C-terminal and central repeats show slightly different structural arrangements, with N- and C- terminal repeats interacting with each other.

label: Importin 13 repeat
def: Importin 13 serves as receptor for nuclear localization signals (NLS) in cargo substrates. It mediates docking of the importin/substrate complex to the nuclear pore complex (NPC). It contains several repeats structurally similar to the HEAT repeat. This entry represents a single repeat unit.

label: Inulin fructotransferase, beta helix repeat
def: This region contains a right-handed parallel β-helix repeat unit found in inulin fructotransferase. This entry includes sequences not found in .

label: Ubiquitous surface protein adhesin repeat
def: The UspA1 head domain is globally similar to other structures of TAA head domains and comprises a trimeric left-handed parallel β-roll, which is formed from 14 repeating 14-16 residue segments that form a ladder of β-strand coils. This entry represents a single repeat unit of the β-ladder.

label: SUVR5, C2H2-type Zinc finger, 3 repeats
def: This entry includes three instances of the Zinc finger C2H2-type. They contain a short β hairpin and an α helix (β/β/α structure), where a single zinc atom is held in place by Cys(2)His(2) (C2H2) residues in a tetrahedral array. Proteins containing this repeats include SUVR5, a histone methyltransferase that functions together with its binding partner LDL1/SWP1 as one of the regulators of flower timing in Arabidopsis.

label: Importin 13, repeat 1
def: Importin 13 has a spiralic structure containing repeats structurally similar to HEAT repeats. It serves as receptor for nuclear localization signals (NLS) in cargo substrates, mediating docking of the importin/substrate complex to the nuclear pore complex (NPC). It contains several repeats structurally similar to the HEAT repeat. This entry represents a single repeat unit.

label: Hexapeptide repeat including loop
def: This entry contains a single hexapeptide repeat unit including a loop between two strands.

label: YdcK Beta solenoid repeat
def: The crystal structure of YDCK from Salmonella cholerae includes a β-solenoid repeat. This entry includes a single repeat unit of YDCK repeat.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF5633
def: This entry represents a 40 residue repeat that is often found in tandem in a small set of bacterial cell surface proteins. The function of this region is not known.

label: PH0542 repeat
def: Repeat found in PH0542 showing some sequence similarity to HEAT repeat.

label: Importin repeat
def: The importin subunit beta-3 has a superhelical structure composed of tandem repeats structurally similar to HEAT repeats. This entry includes a single repeat unit and includes sequences not captured by .

label: Cadmium carbonic anhydrase repeat
def: This entry represents the cadmium carbonic anhydrase repeat unit of the beta-carbonic anhydrase of a marine diatom, that uses both zinc and cadmium for catalysis of the reversible hydration of carbon dioxide for use in inorganic carbon acquisition for photosynthesis (thus being a cambialistic enzyme). Compared with alpha-and gamma-carbonic anhydrases that use three histidines to coordinate the zinc-atom, this beta-carbonic anhydrase has two cysteines and one histidine, and rapidly binds cadmium.

label: Importin 13 repeat 2
def: Importin 13 serves as receptor for nuclear localization signals (NLS) in cargo substrates. It mediates docking of the importin/substrate complex to the nuclear pore complex (NPC). It contains several repeats structurally similar to the HEAT repeat. This Pfam entry represents a single repeat unit.

label: Tetratricopeptide-like repeat
def: This entry includes outlying tetratricopeptide-like repeats (two repeat units) that are not matched by .

label: ALMS repeat
def: This entry contains a single repeat unit of approximately 47 amino acids. It is found in Centrosome-associated protein ALMS1 and homologues.

label: Homing endonuclease associated repeat
def: Homing endonucleases are found in bacteria and viruses and catalyze the hydrolysis of genomic DNA within the cells that synthesize them. This entry represents a 60 residue repeat of unknown function found associated with homing endonuclease domains.

label: Nitrous oxide reductase, propeller repeat 1
def: Nitrous oxide reductases usually contain a seven-bladed β-propeller domain with external short α-helices. This entry represents a single blade of the propeller, with imperfect α-helix, usually at the C terminus of the repeat region.

label: Chromosome region maintenance repeat
def: Chromosome region maintenance 1 or exportin 1 mediates the nuclear transport of proteins bearing a leucin-rich nuclear export signal (NES). It contains helical repeats that are structurally similar to HEAT repeats, but share little sequence similarity with them. N-terminal, C-terminal and central repeats show slightly different structural arrangements, with N- and C- termini repeats interacting with each other. This entry represents the central repeats of CRM1.

label: Nitrous oxide reductase, propeller repeat 2
def: Nitrous oxide reductases usually contain a seven-bladed β-propeller domain with external short α-helices. This entry represents a single blade of the propeller, without α-helical insertion.

label: Vesicle tethering protein p115, armadillo tether-repeat
def: This entry represents the armadillo-like tether-repeat of vesicle tethering protein p115. This entry contains a single copy of the repeat unit.

label: Exportin-1, repeat 2
def: Exportin 1 (Exp1) mediates the nuclear transport of proteins bearing a leucin-rich nuclear export signal (NES). It contains helical repeats that are structurally similar to HEAT repeats, but share little sequence similarity with them. N-, C-terminal and central repeats show slightly different structural arrangements, with N- and C- terminal repeats interacting with each other.

label: Adenomatous polyposis coli (APC) repeat
def: Adenomatous polyposis coli contains an armadillo repeat and uses its highly conserved surface groove to recognize the APC-binding region (ABR) of Asef. This entry represents a single repeat unit of the Armadillo region.

label: Isopullulanase beta-solenoid repeat
def: This isopullulanase (IPU) and dextranase repeat unit includes three (or one long and one short) parallel β-strands. The repeat region as a whole folds into a β-helix, known as β-solenoid.

label: CD180, leucine-rich repeat
def: This entry represents one repeat unit found in CD180 from mammals. CD180 may cooperate with MD-1 and TLR4 to mediate the innate immune response to bacterial lipopolysaccharide (LPS) in B-cells.

label: Leucine-rich repeat unit
def: This Pfam entry includes some leucine-rich repeats (LRRs) that fail to be detected with . This entry represents one repeat unit.

label: Ribonuclease inhibitor, leucine rich repeat cap
def: Leucine-rich repeats are composed of a β-α unit. This repeat is found as capping unit (N- or C- terminal of the repeat region) of ribonuclease inhibitors.

label: Phage spike trimer 2
def: This entry includes some phage spike repeats that fail to be detected model.

label: PlcR, tetratricopeptide repeat
def: The bacterial PIcR helix-turn-helix transcription factor includes five TPR units of different lengths. This entry represents the central, medium-sized TPR repeat.

label: Importin repeat 6
def: The importin subunit beta-3 has a superhelical structure composed of tandem repeats structurally similar to HEAT repeats. This entry represents two consecutive repeat units.

label: Dextranase, beta solenoid repeat
def: The crystal structures of Dex49A from Penicillium minioluteum and of ATCC9642 isopullulanase from Aspergillus niger include β-solenoid repeats, sharing structural similarities. This entry includes a single repeat unit of the repeat regions.

label: DUF4458 domain-containing protein, leucine-rich repeat
def: This entry represents the leucine-rich repeat (LRR) found in proteins that also contain the N-terminal DUF4458 domain.

label: Toxin complex C-like repeat
def: Toxin complexes (Tcs) are virulence factors of many bacteria which are composed of bacterial protein toxins that form large, multisubunit complexes. Tcs mode of action is to perforate the host membrane and transfer toxic enzymes into the host cell. Tc protein subunits can be classified as either type A, B or C. This entry describes a single repeat unit, as found in TcC-like proteins such as toxin subunits YenC1 and 2 from Yersinia entomophaga.

label: Importin repeat 4
def: The importin subunit beta-3 has a superhelical structure composed of tandem repeats structurally similar to HEAT repeats. This entry includes a single repeat unit.

label: QPE repeat
def: This repeat is found in a group of bacterial cell surface proteins with an N-terminal collagen binding domain. We have named this repeat QPE after the most conserved sequence motif.

label: KNL1 MELT repeat
def: The outer kinetochore protein scaffold KNL1 is crucial for a correct chromosome segregation during mitosis and meiosis. The main characteristic of KNL1 is the presence of repeats containing MELT (Met-Glu-Leu-Thr) motifs which are phosphorylated and critical for the BUB1-BUB3 dimer recruitment. BUB1-BUB3 dimer regulates chromosome biorientation and the spindle assembly checkpoint. This entry mainly represents vertebrate proteins although MELT motifs are found much more widely.

label: Plasmodium repeat of length 46
def: This repeat is found in the products of 2 genes in Plasmodium species. It is found in no other organisms.

label: Repeats of unknown function DUF5649
def: This entry represents a series of potential β-helix repeats found in a variety of putative bacterial adhesin proteins.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF5650
def: This entry represents a repeat region found in filamentous hemagglutinin proteins from various bacteria. This entry may contain a β helix structure.

label: EsV-1-7 cysteine-rich motif
def: The EsV-1-7 repeat is a cysteine-rich motif of unknown function. The motif was originally identified in the Ectocarpus "immediate upright" protein, which has an EsV-1-7 domain that contains five EsV-1-7 repeats. The name is derived from the Ectocarpus virus EsV-1 protein EsV-1-7, which possesses six EsV-1-7 repeats. Ectocarpus has a large family of EsV-1-7 domain proteins with between one and 19 copies of the motif (C-X4-C-X16-C-X2-H-X12). In addition to brown algae, EsV-1-7 domain proteins hav…

label: ALTTAQ repeat
def: This repeat is called after the short repeat ALTTAQ found in uncharacterised proteins mostly from Proteobacteria.

label: Wolframin, Sel1-like repeat
def: This entry represents the N-terminal Sel1-like repeats found in the wolframin protein. This region has been linked to calmodulin-binding, suggesting these repeats may mediate a protein interaction. Wolframin, a multi-pass membrane protein found in the endoplasmic reticulum, is expressed by the Wolfram syndrome 1 gene (WFS1). The detailed molecular function of the protein is not known, but it is believed to participate, at least in part, in the regulation of cellular calcium homeostasis by modul…

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF5907
def: This entry represents protein repeats whose length varies between 50 and 80 residues. It is found in a variety of virus and bacteria. It may be distantly related to minor tail protein found in phages.

label: Putative pectate lyase-like adhesive domain
def: This putative domain is found at the N-terminal of a large number of bacterial proteins. Its adhesive function was described in Lactobacillus reuteri serine-rich repeat proteins. Structure predictions show it is composed of a β-solenoid repeat related to Pectate lyase.

label: Tra1, HEAT repeat ring region
def: This entry represents part of the Tra1 protein composed of α-solenoid repeats that form a ring region. This entry includes transcription-associated protein 1 (Tra1) from yeast and TRRAP from mammals. Budding yeast Tra1 is a subunit of SAGA and NuA4 histone acetyltransferase complexes. Human TRRAP is an adapter protein found in various multiprotein chromatin complexes with histone acetyltransferase activity (HAT). This entry also includes Tra2 which is a component of the NuA4 histone H4/H2A acet…

label: Tra1, HEAT repeat central region
def: This entry represents part of the Tra1 protein composed of α-solenoid repeats that forms the central region. This is named as central due to its position relative to the ring region. This entry includes transcription-associated protein 1 (Tra1) from yeast and TRRAP from mammals. Budding yeast Tra1 is a subunit of SAGA and NuA4 histone acetyltransferase complexes. Human TRRAP is an adapter protein found in various multiprotein chromatin complexes with histone acetyltransferase activity (HAT). Th…

label: Laa1/Sip1/HEATR5-like, HEAT repeat region
def: This entry represents a region consisting of HEAT repeats that is found in a group of eukaryotic proteins, including HEAT repeat-containing protein 5A/B (HEATR5A/B) from vertebrates, Pof6 interactor protein 1 (SIP1), AP-1 accessory protein LAA1 from yeast and SWEETIE from Arabidopsis thaliana.

label: E motif
def: This entry represents the E motif (also known as E1) found in plant pentatricopeptide repeat (PPR) proteins. These proteins contain a DYW deaminase domain required for RNA editing, a process that deaminates specific cytidines to uridines. This motif, together with the E motif, precedes the DYW domain and, although their role is not clear, they are essential in the RNA editing reaction. The E/E motifs may contain two degenerate PPR motifs that could be involved protein binding.

label: E2 motif
def: This is the E + motif (also known as E2) found in some plant pentatricopeptide repeat (PPR) proteins. PPRs contain a C-terminal DYW deaminase domain required for RNA editing, a process that deaminates specific cytidines to uridines. This motif, together with the E motif , precedes the DYW domain and, although their role is not clear, they are essential in the RNA editing reaction. The E/E motifs may contain two degenerate PPR motifs that could be involved in protein binding.

label: Flagellar Assembly Protein A, beta solenoid domain
def: This entry represents the C-terminal β-solenoid domain of FapA (flagellar assembly protein A, ) from Vibrio vulnificus and its homologues. The synthesis of flagella allows bacteria to respond to chemotaxis by facilitating motility. Studies examining the role of FapA show that the loss or delocalization of FapA results in a complete failure of the flagellar biosynthesis and motility in response to glucose mediated chemotaxis. The polar localization of FapA is required for flagellar synthesis, an…

label: Tetratricopeptide Repeats-Sensor
def: This entry represents a sensor domain consisting of 7 TPR repeats forming a tightly-wound solenoid structure harbouring a deep central pocket. The TPR-S binding pocket is lined with several conserved aromatic and polar residues predicted to bind a NAD -derived nucleotide in prokaryotic NAD -derived nucleotide-activated effector conflict systems. It has been acquired at the base of the choanoflagellate-animal lineage as a core component of the ASK signalosome.

label: Polycomb protein SCMH1, first MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the first MBT repeat of SCMH1 and similar proteins specific to vertebrates. MTB repeats are protein modules that recognise methylated lysine residues on histones. Unlike other MTB repeats, this repeat does not contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Sex comb on midleg homologue 1 (SCMH1) is the mammal homologue of Drosophila Sex comb on midleg (Scm). It is a component of PRC1 (Polycomb repressive complex 1), which acts as an E3 ubiquitin l…

label: Polycomb protein SCMH1, second MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the second MBT repeat of SCMH1 and similar proteins specific to vertebrates. MTB repeats are protein modules that recognise methylated lysine residues on histones. This repeat contains a semi-aromatic cage to accommodate methylated lysine residues and show specificity towards the lower methylation states of lysines. Sex comb on midleg homologue 1 (SCMH1) is the mammal homologue of Drosophila Sex comb on midleg (Scm). It is a component of PRC1 (Polycomb repressive complex 1…

label: Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 1, third MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the third MBT repeat of SFMBT1, which does not contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 1 (SFMBT1), is a transcriptional repressor protein that selectively binds the N-terminal tail of histone H3 and functions as a chromatin reader in regulating epigenetic silencing and the myogenic gene program. It mediates the recruitment of corepressor complexes to target genes, followed by chromatin compaction and…

label: Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 1, second MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the third MBT repeat of SFMBT1, which contains a partially conserved semi-aromatic cage. Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 1 (SFMBT1), is a transcriptional repressor protein that selectively binds the N-terminal tail of histone H3 and functions as a chromatin reader in regulating epigenetic silencing and the myogenic gene program. It mediates the recruitment of corepressor complexes to target genes, followed by chromatin compaction and repression of transcription. SFM…

label: Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 2, first MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the first MBT repeat of SFMBT2, which contains a partially conserved semi-aromatic cage. Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 2 (SFMBT2) is a transcriptional repressor of the HOXB13 gene. It is required for trophoblast maintenance and placenta development. It also negatively regulates cell migration and invasion in prostate cancer cells. SFMBT2 has been identified as a novel regulator of SOX9 expression in human chondrocytes, in which decreased levels of SFMBT2 contribut…

label: Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 2, third MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the third MBT repeat of SFMBT2, which does not contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 2 (SFMBT2) is a transcriptional repressor of the HOXB13 gene. It is required for trophoblast maintenance and placenta development. It also negatively regulates cell migration and invasion in prostate cancer cells. SFMBT2 has been identified as a novel regulator of SOX9 expression in human chondrocytes, in which decr…

label: Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 2, fourth MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the fourth MBT repeat of SFMBT2, which does not contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Scm-like with four MBT domains protein 2 (SFMBT2) is a transcriptional repressor of the HOXB13 gene. It is required for trophoblast maintenance and placenta development. It also negatively regulates cell migration and invasion in prostate cancer cells. SFMBT2 has been identified as a novel regulator of SOX9 expression in human chondrocytes, in which dec…

label: Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 2, first MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the first MBT repeat of L3MBTL2, which contains a partially conserved semi-aromatic cage. Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 2 (L3MBTL2) is a putative Polycomb group (PcG) protein that binds to monomethylated and dimethylated 'Lys-20' on histone H4 and also binds histone H3 peptides which are monomethylated or dimethylated on 'Lys-4', 'Lys-9' or 'Lys-27'. L3MBTL2 orchestrates ubiquitin signalling by dictating the sequential recruitment of RNF8 and RNF168 after DNA…

label: Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 2, second MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the second MBT repeat of L3MBTL2, which does not seem to contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 2 (L3MBTL2) is a putative Polycomb group (PcG) protein that binds to monomethylated and dimethylated 'Lys-20' on histone H4 and also binds histone H3 peptides which are monomethylated or dimethylated on 'Lys-4', 'Lys-9' or 'Lys-27'. L3MBTL2 orchestrates ubiquitin signalling by dictating the sequential…

label: Polycomb protein Sfmbt, first MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the first MBT repeat of Sfmbt from Drosophila melanogaster (dSfmbt) and similar sequences from arthropods. It contains a partially conserved semi-aromatic cage. Polycomb protein Sfmbt from Drosophila melanogaster (dSfmbt) is a Polycomb group repressor involved in epigenetic regulation of gene expression. dSfmbt contains four MBT (malignant brain tumor) repeats. The MBT repeat is a protein module that recognises methylated lysine residues on histones. MBT repeats contain a…

label: Polycomb protein Sfmbt, second MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the second MBT repeat of Sfmbt from Drosophila melanogaster (dSfmbt) and similar sequences from arthropods. It does not seem to contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Polycomb protein Sfmbt from Drosophila melanogaster (dSfmbt) is a Polycomb group repressor involved in epigenetic regulation of gene expression. dSfmbt contains four MBT (malignant brain tumor) repeats. The MBT repeat is a protein module that recognises methylated lysine res…

label: Polycomb protein Sfmbt, third MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the third MBT repeat of Sfmbt from Drosophila melanogaster (dSfmbt) and similar sequences from arthropods. It does not seem to contain a semi-aromatic cage and may not bind methylated lysine residues. Polycomb protein Sfmbt from Drosophila melanogaster (dSfmbt) is a Polycomb group repressor involved in epigenetic regulation of gene expression. dSfmbt contains four MBT (malignant brain tumor) repeats. The MBT repeat is a protein module that recognises methylated lysine resi…

label: Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 1, first MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the first MBT repeat of L3MBTL1, which contains the semi-aromatic cage and may bind methylated lysine residues. Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 1 (L3MBTL1), is a Polycomb group (PcG) protein that functions as a 'reader' of a network of post-translational modifications by specifically recognizing and binding mono- and dimethyllysine residues on target proteins. It acts as a chromatin compaction factor by binding mono- and dimethylated histone H1b/HIST1H1E at 'Ly…

label: Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 1, third MBT repeat
def: This entry represents the third MBT repeat of L3MBTL1, which contains the semi-aromatic cage and may bind methylated lysine residues. Lethal(3)malignant brain tumor-like protein 1 (L3MBTL1), is a Polycomb group (PcG) protein that functions as a 'reader' of a network of post-translational modifications by specifically recognizing and binding mono- and dimethyllysine residues on target proteins. It acts as a chromatin compaction factor by binding mono- and dimethylated histone H1b/HIST1H1E at 'Ly…

label: DUF11 repeat
def: This entry represents a conserved repeat of about 53 amino acids that is part of the DUF11 domain ( ). Examples containing this repeat include a 132-residue region found repeated in three of the five longest proteins of Bacillus anthracis, a 131-residue repeat in a cell wall-anchored protein of Enterococcus faecalis (Streptococcus faecalis), and a 120-residue repeat in Methanobacterium sp. A similar region is found in some Chlamydia trachomatis outer membrane proteins. In Methanothermobacter sp…

label: LktA-like tandem repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat, about 53 amino acid residues in length, found in tandem copies in a number of bacterial proteins. In some of them, these tandem repeats may comprise most of the entire protein length. The best characterised protein containing this repeat is the leukotoxin LktA of Fusobacterium necrophorum. Leukotoxin (Lkt) is a secreted protein encoded by a tricistronic operon (lktBAC) and it is the major virulence factor of F. necrophorum causing apoptosis and necrosis of bovine…

label: Dumpy, DPY module
def: This entry represents the DPY repeat module described in dumpy from Drosophila melanogaster ( ), a gigantic extracellular matrix protein that contains epidermal growth factor (EGF) modules interspersed with DPY modules. The DPY module forms a compact fold consisting of a small N-terminal 3-10 helix packed against a double-stranded antiparallel disulphide-stabilised β-sheet motif capable of linking end-to-end with EGF modules to form a fibre.

label: Huntingtin, N-terminal, HEAT repeats 1
def: This entry represents the first region of HEAT repeats from Huntingtin (Htt), which is part of the N-terminal HEAT domain. It is separated from the rest of the domain ( ) by an unstructured region. Htt is expressed in many tissues with various expression levels and is involved in a number of cellular functions. However, its exact function is not yet clear. An abnormal polyglutamine (polyQ) expansion between 1-90aa at the very N-terminal causes Huntington's disease (HD), a progressive autosomal-…

label: Huntingtin, bridge
def: Huntingtin (HTT) is expressed in many tissues, with various expression levels and is involved in a number of cellular functions. However, its exact function is not yet clear. An an abnormal polyglutamine (polyQ) expansion in this protein causes Huntington's disease (HD) a progressive autosomal-dominant neurodegenerative disease characterised by an uncontrolled motor movement known as chorea, cognitive disorder and depression. HTT consists of multiple HEAT repeats organised in N-terminal (formed…

label: Huntingtin, C-terminal HEAT repeats
def: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of Huntingtin (Htt), which consists of HEAT repeats. Htt is expressed in many tissues with various expression levels and is involved in a number of cellular functions. However, its exact function is not yet clear. An an abnormal polyglutamine (polyQ) expansion in this protein causes Huntington's disease (HD) a progressive autosomal-dominant neurodegenerative disease characterised by an uncontrolled motor movement known as chorea, cognitive disorder an…

label: LED repeat
def: This entry represents a six residue tandem repeat found in Cerebellar degeneration-related antigen 1 (CDR1) from human. It is rich in Glu and Asp and usually flanked at the N terminus by hydrophobic residues (Leu/Phe) and on the C terminus by an aliphatic residue followed by and Asp residue.

label: WKTW repeat
def: This repeat is found in five copies at the C terminus of Cerebellar degeneration-related antigen 1 (CDR1) from human ( ) and related chordate proteins. It has the conserved sequence motif WKTWI.

label: Ice-binding protein, beta roll
def: The grass Lolium perenne produces an ice-binding protein (LpIBP) that helps this perennial tolerate freezing by inhibiting the recrystallization of ice. Ice-binding proteins (IBPs) are also produced by freeze-avoiding organisms to halt the growth of ice and are better known as antifreeze proteins (AFPs). The ice-binding site (IBS) is formed by a flat β-sheet on one surface of the β-roll. This region folds as a left-handed β-helix. Each of the eight beta-helical coils is composed of two seven-re…

label: RTX toxin, C-terminal repeat
def: This entry represents a short repeat found near the C-terminal of some RTX toxins such as MARTX ( ). AlphaFold models suggest that this repeat forms a β-solenoid structure. These repeats contain a GXGXD motif in each repeat which is similar to that found in the RTX calcium-binding nonapeptide repeat ( ). This suggests that these repeats may also be required for translocation via the type I secretion system.

label: Cellophane-induced 1 repeat
def: Venturia inaequalis is a hemi-biotrophic fungus that causes scab disease of apple. A recently-identified gene from this fungus, cin1 (cellophane-induced 1), is up-regulated over 1000-fold in planta and considerably on cellophane membranes, and encodes a cysteine-rich secreted protein of 523 residues with eight imperfect tandem repeats of ~60 amino acids.

label: Choline-binding repeat
def: This entry represents probable choline-binding cell wall repeats.

label: Pre-mRNA cleavage complex 2 protein Pcf11, RFEGP repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat region found in Pre-mRNA cleavage complex 2 protein Pcf11 from human ( ) and similar proteins from vertebrates. This repeat is about thirteen amino acids with a high content of proline and glycine residues, however, it varies in length among the sequences included in this entry. These repeats contain the highly conserved RFE/DG motif, conserved in PCF11 orthologues in vertebrates but not in Drosophila, C. elegans, or yeast. It has been described that human PCF11 h…

label: Nuclear pore complex-interacting protein B13-like, MII repeat
def: This entry represents the MII repeat found in a group of proteins specific to Homo sapiens, including Nuclear pore complex-interacting protein family member B13 (NPB13), NPB12, NPB11, NPB3, NPB4 and NPB5. These repeats may arrange in a β-solenoid structure. Members of this entry have the repeated sequence MIISRHLPSV.

label: PGRS repeats
def: This entry represents a region of repeats present in a group of proteins from Mycobacteriales named PE_PGRSs, with members implicated in tuberculosis (TB) pathogenesis. Proteins of this family show a modular structure: the N-terminal PE domain ( ), highly homologous to the domain found in tens of other PE proteins and whose structure has been already solved by crystallography; a C-terminal domain that is unique for each protein; and a central polymorphic glycine-rich repeat (PGRS) that varies i…

label: FAM186A, PQQAQ repeat
def: This entry represents a short repeat found in protein FAM186A from human ( ) and related sequences from vertebrates. These repeats form a long α-solenoid structure, in which each repeat forms a single α-helix, characterised by PQQAQ sequence.

label: TELO2-interacting protein 1, middle region repeats
def: This entry includes TELO2-interacting protein 1 from yeast and its homologues from mammals. Human TTI1 is a regulator of the DNA damage response (DDR). It is part of the TTT complex that is required to stabilize protein levels of the phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase-related protein kinase (PIKK) family proteins. The TTT complex is involved in the cellular resistance to DNA damage stresses. Together with the TTT complex and HSP90, participates in the proper folding of newly synthesized PIKKs. Membe…

label: Testis-expressed protein 13C/D, MVPLGDSxSHS repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat found in TEX13C and TEX13D (Testis-expressed proteins) from human and similar proteins from mammals. These repeats have the conserved pattern MVPLGDSxSHS.

label: UV-stimulated scaffold protein A, N-terminal alpha-solenoid repeats
def: This entry represents the N-terminal set of alpha solenoid repeats found in UV-stimulated scaffold protein A (UVSSA) and related proteins from eukaryotes. UVSSA is involved in transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair, a mechanism that rapidly removes RNA polymerase II-blocking lesions from the transcribed strand of active genes.

label: Reelin, subrepeat B
def: Reelin is an extracellular matrix serine protease that plays a role in layering of neurons in the cerebral cortex and cerebellum. It regulates microtubule function in neurons and neuronal migration. It binds to the extracellular domains of lipoprotein receptors VLDLR and LRP8/APOER2 which induces tyrosine phosphorylation of DAB1 and modulation of TAU phosphorylation. Reelin contains 8 tandemly repeated units, each of which is composed of two highly similar subrepeats of 150-190 amino acids (sub…

label: Adhesin AWP2-like, C-terminal YTTT repeat
def: This entry represents a short sequence repeat found at the C terminus of Adhesin AWP2 from the yeast Candida glabrata. AlphaFold predicts this repeat to adopt a beta hairpin structure. This repeat is found in multiple copies in a range of fungal species.

label: Suprabasin, GxHH repeat
def: Suprabasin (SBSN) is involved in epidermal differentiation and may play a role in skin barrier formation. It has been proposed to be a component of the cornified envelope, an essential structure for skin protection. Its exact function is not yet clear; it has been associated with multiple diseases such as cancer and autoimmune disorders. It is a secreted molecule that may induce cellular signalling (AKT, WNT/beta-catenin, and/or p38MAPK signalling) and processes (migration, proliferation, neova…

label: Polyglycine hydrolase-like, tandem repeat
def: Polyglycine hydrolase from Fusarium vanettenii is a serine-type endopeptidase that cleaves Gly-Gly bonds in the polyglycine linker of host plant class IV chitinases to disrupt their chitin-binding, and thereby plays a role in lowering the defence responses of the host to fungi. It is formed by two distinct domains that are connected by a linker. The N-terminal domain is comprised of five nearly identical structural repeats consisting of three antiparallel β-strands and an α-helix (this entry re…

label: Antifreeze protein repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat found in a small family of bacterial proteins involved in stopping the formation of ice crystals at low temperatures (Antifreeze proteins, AFPs), including from Marinomonas primoryensis. The structure folds as a Ca(2+)-bound parallel β-helix with an extensive array of ice-like surface waters that are anchored via hydrogen bonds directly to the polypeptide backbone and adjacent side chains.

label: Properdin, thrombospondin type 1 repeat 0
def: This entry represents the N-terminal thrombospondin type I repeat (TSR-0) found in human Properdin (CFP) and similar proteins from vertebrates. Properdin is a glycoprotein constructed from a common pool of structure units or modules, which are homologous to the thrombospondin type 1 repeat, TSR. It is a positive regulator of the complement system that stabilises the alternative pathway C3-convertase C3bBb. Properdin also inhibits the factor H-mediated cleavage of C3b by factor I. In addition, t…

label: Plectin-like, spectrin-like repeat
def: This entry represents a spectrin repeat found in human Plectin (PCN) and similar animal proteins. PCN interlinks intermediate filaments with microtubules and microfilaments and anchors intermediate filaments to desmosomes or hemidesmosomes.

label: WD repeat-containing protein 93 beta-propeller
def: This entry represents the β-propeller of human WD repeat-containing protein 93 (WDR93, ) and related metazoan proteins. Its function is unknown.

label: Streptococcal M protein, D repeats
def: This entry represents the entire D repeats found in the streptococcal M protein family. M proteins belong to a family of fibrous streptococcal surface proteins that interact with the immune system of the host and represent major virulence factors due to their antiphagocytic activities. These antiphagocytic properties are explained by binding to a variety of different host proteins, with the most prominent one being fibrinogen. Another hypothesis relies on the M-protein-mediated formation of lar…

label: Streptococcal M proteins C repeat
def: This entry represents the entire C repeat found in the streptococcal M protein family. M proteins belong to a family of fibrous streptococcal surface proteins that interact with the immune system of the host and represent major virulence factors due to their antiphagocytic activities. These antiphagocytic properties are explained by binding to a variety of different host proteins, with the most prominent one being fibrinogen. Another hypothesis relies on the M-protein-mediated formation of larg…

label: Sialidase A repeat
def: This entry represents two tandem copies of a typically 17-amino acid repeat with a characteristic EXPEF or EXPEY motif, found mostly in surface-bound glycoside or peptide hydrolases with LPXTG motifs for covalent anchoring on the cell wall from firmicutes. It occurs in the C-terminal region of the Sialidase A from Streptococcus pneumoniae (NanA), where this region is not described as either catalytic or carbohydrate-binding, but instead as P/G/S/T-rich and repeat-containing. Multiple copies of…

label: Surface cell antigen Sca2 helical repeat
def: Sca2 (surface cell antigen 2) is the only bacterial protein known to promote both actin filament nucleation and profilin-dependent elongation, mimicking eukaryotic formins to assemble actin comet tails for Rickettsia motility. Sca2's functional mimicry of formins is achieved through a unique mechanism. Unlike formins, Sca2 is monomeric, but has N- and C-terminal repeat domains (NRD and CRD) that interact with each other for processive barbed-end elongation. The crystal structure of NRD reveals…

label: Properdin, thrombospondin type 1 C-terminal
def: This represents the C-terminal thrombospondin type I repeat (TSR_C) found in human Properdin (CFP) and similar proteins from vertebrates. Properdin is a glycoprotein constructed from a common pool of structure units or modules, which are homologous to the thrombospondin type 1 repeat, TSR. It is a positive regulator of the complement system that stabilises the alternative pathway C3-convertase C3bBb. Properdin also inhibits the factor H-mediated cleavage of C3b by factor I. In addition, this pr…

label: Mucin-20 repeat
def: This entry represents a short repeat found in human Mucin-20 (MUC-20) and similar sequences from primates. MUC-20 may regulate the MET signalling cascade and seems to decrease the hepatocyte growth factor (HGF)-induced transient MAPK activation. This repeat is predicted to form a β-solenoid structure.

label: Domain of unknown function (DUF7017)
def: This entry represents a domain consisting of TPR repeats found at the N-terminal in a group of uncharacterised sequences predominantly from bacteria, which seems to be related to TOTE conflict systems as they are associated with and .

label: Phage tail fiber repeat
def: This entry represents a lasso-like repeat found in phage tail proteins from tailed bacteriophages and prophages mainly found in flavobacteria.

label: Small ribosomal subunit protein mS39, PPR
def: This entry represents a pentatricopeptide repeat (PPR) section found at the N-terminal of animal Small ribosomal subunit protein mS39 (also known as PPR domain protein 3 (PTCD3)), a mitochondrial RNA-binding protein that has a role in mitochondrial translation.

label: Clathrin heavy-chain repeat
def: This entry represents the CHCR found in the arm region of the Clathrin heavy chain. Clathrin is a triskelion-shaped cytoplasmic protein that polymerises into a polyhedral lattice on intracellular membranes to form protein-coated membrane vesicles. Lattice formation induces the sorting of membrane proteins during endocytosis and organelle biogenesis by interacting with membrane-associated adaptor molecules. Clathrin functions as a trimer, and these trimers, or triskelions, are comprised of three…

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7104
def: This entry includes uncharacterised fungal sequences which consist of repeats that may adopt an α-solenoid structure related to ankyrin repeats. The overall arrangement of these repeats may have a horseshoe conformation similar to that in leucine-rich repeats.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7826
def: This entry represents a family of small uncharacterised bacterial α solenoid proteins.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7899
def: This domain is found in uncharacterised plant proteins. It is predicted to adopt an eight-bladed β-propeller.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7911
def: This domain is found in uncharacterised nematode proteins. It is predicted to fold into a five-bladed β-propeller.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7914
def: This domain is found in uncharacterised proteins mainly from Trypanosomes. It is predicted to fold into a seven-bladed β-propeller.

label: PEP5/VPS11, clathrin heavy-chain repeat
def: This entry represents the clathrin heavy-chain repeat (CHCR) repeat found in Pep5-like zinc finger protein from Schizosaccharomyces pombe (also known as Vps11) and its ortologues from human, mouse and A. thaliana Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 11 (VPS11). Vacuolar protein sorting protein 11 (vps11) aggregates with other Vps proteins to form the homotypic fusion and protein sorting complex (HOPS), which promotes the fusion of late endosomes and autophagosomes with lysosomes. Pep5 (a…

label: WD repeat-containing protein 35, TPR repeats
def: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in human WD repeat-containing protein 35 (WDR35), a component of the IFT complex A (IFT-A), which is involved in ciliogenesis and ciliary protein trafficking. Members of this entry are mainly found in animals.

label: ATM-like, TPR repeat
def: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in Serine/threonine-protein kinase ATM from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. ATM acts as a DNA damage sensor upon double strand breaks (DSBs), apoptosis and genotoxic stresses such as ionizing ultraviolet A light (UVA). It regulate DNA damage response mechanism through recognising the substrate consensus sequence [ST]-Q and phosphorylates 'Ser-139' of histone variant H2AX/H2AFX at double strand breaks (DSBs). On DNA da…

label: CFAP46, N-terminal TPR repeat
def: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found at the N-terminal in human Cilia- and flagella-associated protein 46 (CFAP46) and similar eukaryotic proteins. CFAP46 is a part of the central apparatus of the cilium axoneme and plays a role in cilium movement and thereby cell motility. The tetratricopeptide repeat region (TPR) is a structural motif present in a wide range of proteins. It mediates protein-protein interactions and the assembly of multiprotein complexes. The TPR motif…

label: Decapeptide repeat
def: This entry corresponds to a region composed of a 10 residue repeat that forms an α-helical solenoid structure found in uncharacterised eukaryotic proteins and putative IcmE proteins in bacteria.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7951
def: This entry describes a repeat of unknown function found at the C-terminal of uncharacterised proteins mainly from animals. It displays a TPR fold.

label: Transportin-3/Importin-13, second TPR domain
def: This entry represents the second TPR domain found in Transportin-3 (TNPO3) from animals, as well as orthologues MTR10 and uncharacterised protein YNR7 from yeast and MOS14 from plant. This entry also includes Importin-13 (IPO3) from animals. This domain is involved in the binding to the globular Ran protein in TNOP3. In IPO13, this domain is involved in the protein interaction with Ubc9 for nuclear import and with eIF1A for nuclear import. These proteins are involved in transport of target prot…

label: Transportin-3/Importin-13, third TPR domain
def: This entry represents the third TPR domain found in Transportin-3 (TNPO3) from animals, as well as orthologues MTR10 and uncharacterised protein YNR7 from yeast and MOS14 from plants. This entry also includes Importin-13 (IPO3) from animals. This domain is part of TPR structure that binds proteins for nuclear transport. These proteins are involved in transport of target proteins into the nucleus. TNPO3 mediates the nuclear import of splicing factor serine/arginine (SR) proteins, such as RBM4, S…

label: LA_2272-like repeat
def: This entry represents a 15-amino acid repeat. Numbers of repeats in proteins vary. Notable surface-exposed proteins with this repeat domain include lipoproteins LA_2272 (Q8F3X7) and LA_2273 (Q8F3X6) from Leptospira interrogans and VC2662 (Q9KNR9) from Vibrio cholerae.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF7957
def: This domain is found in uncharacterised protein from Halogeometricum borinquense (Hbor_03000) and related prokaryotic proteins. This protein is found next to a toxic gene Hbor_02990 and is likely a immunity protein which is able to prevent self-intoxication. This domain is predicted to fold into three four-stranded beta-sheets that pack in a similar way as blades in beta-propellers.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF8034
def: This entry represents a family of probable enzymes found in a range of bacteria. Shows structural similarity to Mannosyl-oligosaccharide alpha-1,2-mannosidase.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF8079
def: This domain is found in N-terminal in uncharacterised eukaryotic proteins. It is predicted to be composed of HEAT repeats. It is often found in combination with .

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF8137
def: This entry represents a domain from a family of uncharacterised proteins found in archaea. The proteins in this family are predominantly found in the class Halobacteria, which includes genera such as Halorubrum, Natronorubrum, and Halovenus. The proteins containing this domain vary in length, with sequences ranging from approximately 59 to 347 amino acids. The structure of this domain adopts an alpha solenoid.

label: Transportin-3/Importin-13, fourth TPR domain
def: This entry represents the third TPR domain found in Transportin-3 (TNPO3) from animals, as well as orthologues MTR10 and uncharacterised protein YNR7 from yeast and MOS14 from plants. This entry also includes Importin-13 (IPO3) from animals. This domain interacts with the RS region of the ASF protein for nuclear transport. These proteins are involved in transport of target proteins into the nucleus. TNPO3 mediates the nuclear import of splicing factor serine/arginine (SR) proteins, such as RBM4…

label: Bacterial TSP3 repeat
def: This entry contains a novel bacterial thrombospondin type 3 repeat which differs from the typical consensus by containing a glutamate in place of one of the calcium binding aspartate residues.

label: PRP39, C-terminal HAT repeat
def: This entry represents the C-terminal segment of the HAT (Half A TPR) repeat proteins including Pre-mRNA-processing factor 39, SART3, PRP42 and similar proteins. PRP39 is a component of the spliceosome that functions prior to stable branch point recognition by the U1 snRNP particle to facilitate or stabilize the U1 snRNP/5'-splice site interaction and has a direct role in spliceosome assembly. PRP42 from yeast is an essential component of the U1 snRNP particle, which recognises and binds the 5'-…

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF8253
def: This domain is found in uncharacterised proteins mainly from Microbacteriaceae. It contains repetitive QC sequence motif frequently found in the context of NQCN motif. This domain is predicted to fold into an unique β-sandwich formed of these consecutive motifs.

label: LOFI repeat
def: This weakly conserved (low-fidelity, or LOFI) repeat appears primarily in Campylobacteria, but not exclusively, in proteins such as Cj0967, Cj0736, and Cj0735. This entry represents a core region about 23 residues in length of repeats whose lengths vary typically between 35 and 50 residues in length.

label: Acidic double-disulfide repeat
def: The acidic double-disulphide repeat is an Asp-rich repeat with four nearly invariant Cys residues in a repeat length of about 35 amino acids. This repeat can be found in a variety of uncharacterised proteins from bacteria and eukaryotes. It can be found also repeated once or multiple times.

label: Lonely Cys repeat
def: This entry represents a repeat found in Streptomyces. This repeat contains an extremely low cysteine composition, one residue per domain with an essentially invariant position.

label: Repeat of unknown function DUF8500
def: This domain of unknown function is found in a group of bacterial sequences, functionally uncharacterised. It may adopt a β-propeller structure.

label: Tandem large repeat
def: This entry represents a domain of nearly 200 amino acids, found in up to 14 tandem repeats in the C-terminal region of very large protein, in Vibrio parahaemolyticus and related species.
